action.reasoning: Observation: All recent local tests show 0% success with heuristic planner, explored_rate ~0.2-0.35, travel_distance ~800m. The robot moves a lot but explores poorly, indicating it fails to navigate towards frontiers effectively. The best score so far (0.5502) was from a simpler greedy-utility + BFS planner that worked in iteration 13, but the current implementations have bugs that cause early termination or ineffective movement. Analysis: The likely issue is that the planner gets stuck when all node utilities are zero (no visible frontiers) and the fallback mechanism fails because all immediate neighbours are in the tabu list, causing the episode to end prematurely. Also, path reconstruction or graph building may have bugs. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with a different strategy: frontier‑based exploration. Detect frontier cells on the belief map (FREE adjacent to UNKNOWN), choose a target frontier (farthest from robot or random), find the nearest graph node to that frontier, BFS to that node, and move one step along the path. This directly targets unexplored areas and should avoid the zero‑utility deadlock. Implement a robust fallback (if no path, move to random neighbour, no tabu) to ensure the robot never stops early. Test locally on 15 training maps to verify >0 success rate, then submit eval to get a new baseline. If success, use remaining iterations to refine path efficiency (e.g., utility‑weighted target selection).
